articles.title: From Championship Teammates to Finals Rivals—The History in Green
articles.original_title: From championship teammates to Finals rivals—The History in Green
articles.summary: The article revisits the 1971 ABA Finals, where two former Boston Celtics stars faced each other on opposite sides. It highlights how their shared championship background added intrigue to the series and examines the broader historical context of former teammates meeting as opponents.
key_points: 1971 ABA Finals featured two ex‑Celtics players as rivals.
Both players had won NBA titles together before the ABA showdown.
The matchup underscored the fluidity of player movement between leagues in the early 1970s.

articles.title: Lakers Have Reportedly Contacted Thunder About Lu Dort
articles.original_title: Lakers have reportedly called Thunder about Lu Dort
articles.summary: Los Angeles Lakers scouts are said to have reached out to the Oklahoma City Thunder regarding a potential trade for guard Lu Dort. The move would allow the Lakers to leverage their available cap space while adding a defensive specialist to their roster.
key_points: Lakers initiated trade talks with Thunder for Lu Dort.
The trade could help the Lakers use their cap flexibility.
Dort is valued for his perimeter defense and three‑point shooting.

articles.title: Jazz Eye Backup Center Re‑Signature in Busy Free‑Agency Landscape
articles.original_title: Utah Jazz Free Agency: Jazz interested in re-signing backup center
articles.summary: Following the NBA Draft, the Utah Jazz are focusing on several free‑agency decisions, including the possible re‑signing of their backup center. The team aims to solidify depth in the frontcourt while navigating salary‑cap considerations.
key_points: Utah is interested in retaining its backup center for the upcoming season.
The decision comes amid a crowded off‑season roster overhaul after the draft.
Cap management will be a key factor in the Jazz’s free‑agency strategy.

articles.title: Jose Alvarado’s Post‑Championship Celebration Raises Concerns
articles.original_title: Jose Alvarado struggling to keep eight-day post-championship  Knicks drinking pledge
articles.summary: Knicks guard Jose Alvarado has been enjoying the aftermath of New York’s championship run, but his partying habits have drawn attention. The article notes that his behavior may be testing the limits of the team’s eight‑day celebration pledge.
key_points: Alvarado is reportedly indulging heavily in post‑title celebrations.
His activities could conflict with the team’s eight‑day drinking pledge.
The situation highlights the challenges of maintaining discipline after a championship.

articles.title: Mitchell Robinson Honors Community Before Knicks Title Parade
articles.original_title: Mitchell Robinson’s heartwarming gesture with neighborhood on night before Knicks’ title parade
articles.summary: Ahead of the Knicks’ championship parade, forward Mitchell Robinson visited his hometown neighborhood to give back to the people who supported him. The gesture was described as heartfelt and underscored his connection to the community.
key_points: Robinson spent the night before the parade visiting his local community.
He engaged in charitable activities and thanked supporters.
The act was praised as a sincere celebration of the team’s title.
